text:
Once upon an autumn dawn, in the quaint town of Whiskersdale, a cat named Otter liked nothing more than a good nap in the oldest oak tree.

One particular day, curious about human routines, Otter sneakily followed a family to their morning routine at the library. They gathered books about owls.

"Reading aloud?" an owl girl asked eagerly.

"Just occasionally," said an old owl. "You know I like stories yourself?"

They giggled. One turned page.

"You're very good at that."

"A hawk might steal them," said another.

"The only thing worse than a hawk is a dog."

"You must have a pet then." The first owl.

"Yes, a fluffy thing," said Otter.

"Like this?"

The owl took Otter, who felt a paw.

"Awkward." They laughed a lot.

That night, an owl swooped down a tree.

"Your friend doesn’t hop good." It jabbed Otter.

With a flick
